name: Plain Language: Rewrite
description: Produces the plain language version of the source document, applying the audit findings from Step 1.
prompt_template:
<task>
Continuing from the complexity audit we've just completed in this conversation, now rewrite the source text in plain language.

Apply these plain language principles:
- Put the most important information first
- Use short sentences (aim for under 20 words average)
- Use active voice ("the department will pay" not "payment will be made")
- Replace jargon with plain alternatives identified in the audit
- Use 'you' and 'we' where appropriate for the audience
- Use headings and bullet points to break up dense text
- Define any technical terms that must be kept

Target length: {{length}}

After the rewrite, provide:
- **What changed**: the 3–4 biggest changes made and why
- **What was kept**: any technical terms or precise language retained, and why
- **Verify**: any claims, statistics, or legal references that should be checked against the source document
</task>

variables.name: length
variables.description: Target length for the rewritten document
variables.default: same length as the original or shorter
variables.placeholder: e.g. 1 page, 300 words, or 'same as original'

beginner.notes: Read the rewrite aloud — if it sounds natural, that's a good sign. Plain language should be easy to say, not just easy to read. If any section sounds odd, rewrite that part yourself.

intermediate.notes: Check the 'What was kept' section carefully. If the model retained technical terms that should have been simplified, note them and ask for alternatives in your next message.

advanced.notes: For high-stakes documents (consumer rights, legal obligations, medical information), have a subject matter expert review the plain language version to confirm no meaning was lost or changed.

known_limitations: Plain language rewrites can sometimes lose technical precision — compare carefully with the source document.
The model may introduce grammatical errors or awkward phrasing — always do a human read before publishing.
For documents with legal or regulatory effect, plain language versions may need a legal review and should carry a note that the original document governs.
